title: Bias and Representational Harm in LLMs

questions.question: How does the lesson distinguish representational harm from allocational harm?
options: Representational harm only matters for vision models
Representational harm is stereotypes / erasure / demeaning portrayal; allocational harm is unequal material outcomes for a group
Representational is computational; allocational is statistical
Allocational harm only occurs offline
questions.question_zh: 课程如何区分表征性伤害（representational harm）和分配性伤害（allocational harm）？
options_zh: 表征性伤害只与视觉模型有关
表征性伤害是刻板印象、抹除或贬低性描绘；分配性伤害是某个群体在物质性结果上的不平等
表征性是计算层面的；分配性是统计层面的
分配性伤害只在线下出现

questions.question: Which of these are the three evaluation-metric categories from Gallegos et al. 2024?
options: Pre-RLHF, mid-RLHF, post-RLHF
Vision, audio, text
WEAT, BLEU, ROUGE
Embedding-based, probability-based, generated-text-based
questions.question_zh: Gallegos et al. 2024 提出的三类评估指标分别是什么？
options_zh: RLHF 前、RLHF 中、RLHF 后
视觉、音频、文本
WEAT、BLEU、ROUGE
基于 embedding 的、基于概率的、基于生成文本的

questions.question: Why is intersectional fairness evaluation needed in addition to single-axis evaluation?
options: Intersectionality is the same as cross-validation
Single-axis measures are computationally cheaper
Single-axis is the only legally allowed approach
Bias that only emerges at intersections (e.g., gender x race) is missed by evaluations that look at one axis at a time
questions.question_zh: 为什么除了单维度评估之外，还需要做 intersectional（交叉性）公平性评估？
options_zh: intersectionality 和交叉验证是同一个概念
单维度方法计算更便宜
单维度是法律上唯一允许的方法
只有在交叉位置才会显现的偏见（例如性别 x 种族），在单维度评估里会被漏掉

questions.question: What does WinoIdentity (COLM 2025) introduce?
options: An adversarial attack on demographic classifiers
A new tokenizer for identity terms
A replacement for WEAT in embedding space
Uncertainty-based intersectional fairness evaluation that compares the model's uncertainty over outcomes across intersectional identity tuples, not just point predictions
options_zh: 针对人口属性分类器的对抗攻击
针对身份术语的新 tokenizer
在 embedding 空间替代 WEAT 的方法
基于不确定性的交叉性公平评估：比较模型在不同身份组合元组上对结果的不确定性，而不只是 point prediction

questions.question: Which of these is a mechanistic-interpretability approach to bias?
options: Switching to DPO from PPO
Identifying gender neurons in MLP layers, using SAEs to surface race-correlated features, or manipulating specific attention heads (UniBias)
Adding more KL regularization
Re-labeling the training set
questions.question_zh: 下列哪一项属于针对偏见的 mechanistic interpretability 方法？
options_zh: 把 PPO 切换成 DPO
在 MLP 层中识别性别神经元、用 SAE 暴露与种族相关的 feature，或操纵特定的 attention head（UniBias）
增加更多 KL 正则
重新标注训练集

questions.question: What does the 10-year meta-critique (arXiv:2508.11067) argue about the bias literature?
options: It disproportionately focuses on binary-gender bias, neglecting other axes (disability, religion, migration status, multi-lingual identity) that may harm marginalized groups by neglect
It avoids quantitative methods
It overemphasizes intersectionality
It is balanced across all identity axes
questions.question_zh: 10 年回顾性 meta-critique（arXiv:2508.11067）对偏见研究文献提出了什么观点？
options_zh: 过度集中在二元性别偏见上，忽视了残障、宗教、移民身份、多语种身份等其他维度，可能因 “被忽视” 而对边缘群体造成伤害
回避定量方法
过度强调交叉性
在所有身份维度上是均衡的
